title: MCIP RlTransition v1
description: One committed or rejected environment step from a Proof Search RL episode: state -> action -> reward -> next_state, never a packet record reinterpreted as a transition. Consumes the transition contract proposed by llm-driven-proof-search#238 (itself blocked on runtime persistence work in llm-driven-proof-search#231 as of this schema's authoring — see mathcorpus#9). Model-related: carries model_config_hash and redaction-aware public_metadata. Every field that #238/#9 require 'or explicitly mark unavailable' is required at the envelope level but individually nullable, so a producer must make an explicit per-field choice rather than silently omitting a key; missing_field_reasons documents *why* a required field is null for a given record (e.g. a legacy episode that predates #231) instead of leaving the gap unexplained.

episode_id.description: Proof Search episode identifier this step belongs to (matches the episode_id convention already used by packet.verification.episode_id and MCIP attempt_record.episode_id).

problem_version_id.description: Proof Search's own problem-version identifier. Distinct from packet_id: a problem may be versioned in Proof Search before MathCorpus packages a packet for it.

step_index.description: 0-based position of this transition within its episode. tools/mathcorpus/rl_transitions.py checks that an episode's step_index values are contiguous and gap-free.

action.description: Deliberately unconstrained: the action space (tactic text, retrieval query, whole-proof submission, ...) is defined by the producing environment, not MCIP. Non-null is a business rule (tools/mathcorpus/rl_transitions.py), not a schema constraint, since a legacy record may legitimately have none (see missing_field_reasons).

reward.description: Scalar reward for this step. Never silently defaulted to 0 for missing data (llm-driven-proof-search#231 acceptance criteria) -- a null here must be explained in missing_field_reasons unless the record's trust_status/export_eligibility already marks it as a fixture.

outcome.description: Explicit per-step verifier outcome. 'unknown' is the honest placeholder for legacy data (paired with a missing_field_reasons entry), never silently coerced to a success/failure guess.

restriction_profile_id.description: Pins to restriction_profiles/ by record_id, same pattern as proof_variant.restriction_profile_id.

diagnostic_refs.description: Best-effort references (ids or hashes) to diagnostic artifacts for this step, where available. Not required -- absence just means none were captured, distinct from the required-field missing_field_reasons mechanism above.

missing_field_reasons.description: Map of field-name -> honest, human-readable reason a required field above is null on this record (e.g. {"reward": "legacy episode predates reward persistence, see llm-driven-proof-search#231"}). Distinct from model_run.censored_fields[], which marks fields withheld by export policy despite being known.

inline.description: Raw or structured proof-state content when not artifact-referenced. A conformant transition sets exactly one of artifact_hash/inline; this schema does not enforce that cross-field rule (see tools/mathcorpus/rl_transitions.py).
